title: androsace vitaliana subsp. sesleri
intro: Androsace vitaliana subsp. sesleri, also known as Sesleri's rock jasmine or alpine rose, is a flowering plant species in the Primulaceae family. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers are used in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Androsace vitaliana subsp. sesleri include treating various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as being used as an anti-inflammatory agent to reduce swelling and pain. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to various areas of the body.
